_note: New-coven creation wizard. Steps run in order; kind='prose'|'coven'|'choice'. A choice's effects (same vocabulary as scenes) apply only when you Begin, so Back is safe. Each option's `saga` line is written into the Saga under 'The Founding' (second person). Flags (player_*, appointed_*, deed_*, path_*, belief_*, warriors_*) let scenes key off the Archon's backstory. Some choices are abstract (colour, belief); others are concrete deeds (deed, path, warriors) — the KODP sprinkle. TODO: split into fixed + a random pool, and shuffle/subset answers per playthrough.

steps.title: Keeper of the Sacred Flame
body: There was an age when the Sacred Flame burned whole beneath the Secret Tower, and the world was whole with it. But the keepers of that fire went out from the Tower and did not return; the great door fell dark; and slowly, as frost creeps across a windowpane in the night, the world began to forget.

When the Tower’s enemies gathered in that dark, those who had kept the fire would not let the whole of it be taken. They broke the Flame into Embers, and scattered to the far and hidden hearths of the world, each bearing a shard against the ruin. One such Ember came at last to Runehold, and burns there yet, in your hearth-rune. You are its keeper now, the appointed Archon — and should that fire fail, Runehold fails with it, and one more light goes out of the world.
Yet before the first season turns, let it be told who you are: your colour and your coming, and the hard things already done to keep this small fire alight.

steps.title: Your Circle
steps.body: These few keep the Ember with you. They will counsel you and quarrel among themselves; lead them well.

steps.title: Your Colour
steps.prompt: Every Wizard leans toward one of the seven schools. Which do you name your own?

options.label: Red
options.desc: Coin, courts, and the Gilded Reach. Power bought and sold.
options.saga: You claimed the Red for your own — the coin-magic of courts.

options.label: Yellow
options.desc: The bending of time and season. Patience made into power.
options.saga: You claimed the Yellow — the slow magic of time and season.

options.label: Brown
options.desc: Earth, craft, and the practical working. Magic that feeds a hold.
options.saga: You claimed the Brown — plain, practical magic that feeds a hold.

options.label: Green
options.desc: Growing things and the wild. Nurture against the dark.
options.saga: You claimed the Green — the wild magic of growing things.

options.label: Blue
options.desc: Lore and memory; the Bastion and its Athenaeum. Knowledge above all.
options.saga: You claimed the Blue — the lore-magic of the Bastion, and set memory above all.

options.label: Purple
options.desc: Chaos and the unmaking of certainty. Power without a leash.
options.saga: You claimed the Purple — chaos-magic, power without a leash.

options.label: White
options.desc: Order and clarity against the Forgetting. The wall that holds.
options.saga: You claimed the White — the order-magic that holds against the Forgetting.

steps.title: How You Came to Lead
steps.prompt: The seat of Archon was not empty. How did it become yours?

options.label: The dying keeper named you
options.desc: The last Archon gave you the fire with their final breath. The coven honours the choice — and watches to see if it was wise.
options.saga: You came to the seat by the dying keeper's last word, and the coven honoured the choice.

options.label: The coven raised you
options.desc: They chose you from among themselves. Their faith in you is real — and yours to keep or lose.
options.saga: The coven raised you to the seat with open hands.

options.label: You took it by force
options.desc: The old Archon is a Forgotten Soul now, and not by the Flame's choosing. The coven follows — warily.
options.saga: You took the seat by force; the old Archon is a Forgotten Soul, and not by the Flame's will.

steps.title: The Winter the Ember Nearly Died
steps.prompt: Last winter the hearth-rune guttered low, and something had to be given to keep it lit. What did you give?

options.label: You burned the old archives
options.desc: You fed the coven's oldest books to the fire — knowledge traded for warmth, and the fire held.
options.saga: In the winter the Ember guttered low, you burned the old archives to keep it lit — knowledge for warmth.

options.label: You sold a Wizard's service to Red
options.desc: You pledged one of your own to the Gilded Reach for a year, and bought fuel enough with the coin.
options.saga: In the lean winter, you pledged a Wizard's year of service to the Gilded Reach to buy the fire its fuel.

options.label: You let the eldest go cold
options.desc: You gave the fire what warmth there was, and let three of the old die of the frost. You keep their names.
options.saga: In the killing frost, you let three of the eldest go cold rather than let the Ember die — and you keep their names still.

steps.title: Your First Working
steps.prompt: Every Wizard remembers the first magic they ever worked. What was yours?

options.label: A candle that would not go out
options.desc: A small, stubborn flame you lit as a child and could not put out for three days. You have kept fires ever since.
options.saga: Your first working was a candle-flame that would not go out — a small, stubborn light.

options.label: A locked door, opened with a word
options.desc: A door barred against you swung wide at a word you didn't know you knew. Nothing has been quite closed to you since.
options.saga: Your first working was a locked door opened with a single spoken word.

options.label: A wound you closed by will
options.desc: You sealed a dying friend's wound with nothing but your will — or you didn't, and it has driven you ever since.
options.saga: Your first working was a wound closed by will alone — or one that never would close again.

steps.title: What Magic Is
steps.prompt: The wise argue over what magic truly is. What do you believe?

options.label: Memory
options.desc: Magic is the world remembering how it once worked — and it is being forgotten.
options.saga: You hold that magic is the world's own memory, and that it is going out.

options.label: A gift
options.desc: Magic is given, from the Flame or something older, to those who are meant to carry it.
options.saga: You hold that magic is a gift, given to those meant to carry it.

options.label: A tool
options.desc: Magic is a power like any other, to be wielded by whoever is strong enough to hold it.
options.saga: You hold that magic is a tool, for whoever is strong enough to wield it.

steps.title: Steel and Rune
steps.prompt: The last time the Warriors and Runehold met over drawn steel, what did you do?

options.label: You stood with them
options.desc: You fought shoulder to shoulder with the Warriors against a common threat, and the hold held. Steel remembers a debt.
options.saga: The last time steel and rune met at Runehold, you stood shoulder to shoulder with the Warriors, and the hold held.

options.label: You turned them away
options.desc: You met the Warriors at the gate with cold courtesy and no bloodshed, and sent them on their road. They will not forget the slight.
options.saga: The last time the Warriors came to Runehold's gate, you turned them away with cold courtesy and no bloodshed.

options.label: You struck first
options.desc: You did not wait for the first blow. Steel has remembered Runehold ever since — and sharpens for it.
options.saga: The last time steel threatened Runehold, you struck first — and steel has remembered it since.
